insight: Orphan abbreviations persist after tag removal — always trace content references when deleting a definition source

insight: <token_note> is not CC-native — CC reads it as plain text with no special processing; removed 18 instances with zero behavioral impact

insight: 35 files, -82 lines net, 1355 tests passing, 0 regressions — terminology + convention + dead code cleanup in single commit

insight: Content framework에서 컴포넌트 제거 시 blast radius는 src/ 넘어 root docs, user guides, test files까지 확장. grep -ri <entity> . 전체 스캔 필수

insight: 16 files 수정, 5 files 삭제, +12 new tests (42 total context_loader), 1670/1670 passed, 0 regressions
author: ajitta

insight: Video claims 1% of context window for skills budget. Web research found actual mechanism is SLASH_COMMAND_TOOL_CHAR_BUDGET = 15,000 chars (character-based, not percentage). Individual descriptions truncated to ~250 chars in listing.
author: ajitta
context: Source video by AI 엔지니어의 시선 cited SKILL_BUDGET_CONTEXT_PERCENT=0.01 — unverifiable constant name

insight: Triple Repetition (T3) is SuperClaude biggest gap at 3/10. R13 Intent Verification appears only 2x, R18 Necessity Test only 1x. No mechanism to re-inject rules after auto-compaction.
author: ajitta
context: Codebase audit (64 tool calls) mapped all critical rules across 23 agents + core files

insight: context:fork documented in skill-authoring.md but unused in all 5 live skills (confidence-check, ship, simplicity-coach, verbalized-sampling, finishing-a-development-branch). Theory vs practice gap.
author: ajitta
context: Fork serves dual purpose: token efficiency + intentional info asymmetry (per existing auto-memory)

insight: SuperClaude architecture is sound — STRONG on 4/7 Anthropic internal techniques (DO/DON'T, Verification, Turn Strategy, Skills Budget). Gaps are content-level (numeric criteria, enumeration, gotchas) not structural.
author: ajitta
context: Sequential thinking + codebase audit + web research convergent finding

insight: T3 fix: Position 2 (add top-3 critical rules to agent gotchas) is right-sized. Position 3 (hook-based rule reminder) deferred as code change with unclear ROI.
author: ajitta
context: 3 positions: core (exists), agent gotchas (content-only fix), hooks (code change — defer)

insight: Prohibition + form enumeration > prohibition alone. Anthropic enumerates all evasion forms (prose/summary/structured output fabrication). SuperClaude R15 says cite evidence but does not enumerate HOW agents evade.
author: ajitta
context: CC source: do not fabricate in prose, summary, OR structured output — each form explicitly listed

insight: 7 files committed (4 modified + 3 new docs), 626 insertions, 4 deletions. Tests: 1682/1682 pass (1 pre-existing fail). 2 self-reviews ran (37 + 50 tool calls). 0 regressions.
author: ajitta

insight: Self-review catches design-vs-implementation drift: missing R15 examples row and anti_misunderstanding wording inconsistency. When design defines rule text + examples table changes in same section, secondary item is easy to miss.
author: ajitta

insight: Deferred W1 (agent gotchas across 18 agents) and W2 (context:fork on skills) after reviewer challenge: content improvements without measured problems. Start with measurement, not assumption.
author: ajitta
context: W1: no evidence of rule drift in production. W2: current skills dont benefit from fork overhead.

insight: Full brainstorm-to-commit pipeline validated: brainstorm → research → discovery → self-review (corrections) → design → implement → self-review (catch drift) → commit. 2 reviews caught 5 critical + 2 important issues before commit.
author: ajitta
context: ~280K tokens across main + 4 sub-agents. Pipeline prevents factual errors from reaching commit.

insight: W1 Phase 1: 5 agents gotchas added (self-review, system-architect, refactoring-expert, project-manager, root-cause-analyst). Gotchas count 5→10. 720/721 tests pass (1 pre-existing). 7 files changed.
author: ajitta

insight: R06 Scope chosen as secondary gotcha for 3/5 agents (self-review, system-architect, refactoring-expert). Scope creep is the most common cross-cutting agent failure mode, even though R06 was not in the design spec critical rule list (R02, R13, R15, R18).
author: ajitta
context: Design spec template mapped each agent category to one primary rule. R06 added as secondary based on observed agent behavior patterns.

insight: simplicity-guide.md places gotchas before handoff, deviating from agent-authoring.md convention (after handoff, before bounds). backend-architect follows convention correctly. Not corrected to avoid scope creep.
author: ajitta
context: agent-authoring.md says placement: after handoff, before bounds. simplicity-guide has gotchas→handoff→bounds order.

insight: Content-only framework changes (markdown gotchas) have near-zero test risk. All 720 structural tests pass without modification. Plan-to-implementation for content changes takes minutes vs hours for code changes.
author: ajitta
ajitta
insight: W1 Phase 2 selective: 5 agents gotchas added (python-expert, devops-architect, performance-engineer, business-panel-experts, requirements-analyst). Count 10->15. 720/721 tests pass. 7 files committed.

author: ajitta
insight: Phase 2 selective (5/13) over full rollout. Measurement gate impractical (no A/B for compaction drift), token cost trivial (~3 lines/agent), but educators+executors are low-judgment — gotchas have diminishing returns on procedural agents.
context: Original plan said measure Phase 1 first. Pragmatic decision: skip impractical gate, apply to high-value subset only.

author: ajitta
insight: R02 wording must be domain-differentiated even when the rule is identical: "Before implementing" (python) vs "Before configuring" (devops) vs "Before optimizing" (perf). Boilerplate R02 caught by self-review agent in iteration 2.
action: Changed devops-architect R02 from "Before implementing" to "Before configuring...infrastructure isn't already provisioned"

author: ajitta
insight: Self-review loop (--loop iteration 2) caught near-identical R02 wording for python/devops that wouldn't fail any test. Review agents find quality gaps invisible to structural validation.
context: Tests validate structure (tag presence, placement) but not content quality (differentiation, domain specificity).

author: ajitta
insight: R18 Necessity Test applied to requirements-analyst with domain-adapted phrasing: "more complete" replaces "safer/better" as the insufficient justification example. Rule paraphrasing should match agent domain vocabulary.
CLAUDE.md audit: 3 stale claims fixed (MCP 9->8, tests 1787->1807, install chain +2 files). 12 orphan root files deleted (4,209 lines). Score 87->94.
author: ajitta

insight: Glob tool returns different file counts than ls | wc -l on Windows Git Bash — backslash path separators cause undercounting with pipe chains. Glob is the reliable source.
author: ajitta

insight: CLAUDE.md staleness accumulates silently after cleanup commits. MorphLLM removal (e18212f) and test additions were committed but doc counts never updated — no CI or hook enforces CLAUDE.md currency.
author: ajitta

insight: Current content inventory: 33 commands, 23 agents, 5 skills, 7 modes, 8 MCP docs, 4 core configs. Test collection: 1,807. Version: 4.4.1+ajitta.
author: ajitta
ajitta
insight: Authoring rule compliance fix: 57 files, +303/-71 lines. Tests 1266->1267 (frontend-architect fixed). Gotchas 100% coverage across agents/commands/skills
context: Full audit and fix of all 4 authoring rule sets (agent, command, skill, mode)
action: Script-based bulk edit + manual fixes for structural violations

author: ajitta
insight: Sub-agents with bypassPermissions mode still denied Edit tool on Windows — permissions enforced by host, not agent mode. Python script workaround required
context: 4 sub-agent attempts (auto + bypassPermissions) all failed to Edit files. Main conversation Edit works fine.

author: ajitta
insight: maxTurns removed from all 22 agents (R18: system not broken without it, values were untested estimates, CC has built-in limits)
context: Originally added to all 23 agents, then removed after R18 necessity test. Only effort field retained.
action: sed removal of maxTurns line from all agent frontmatter

author: ajitta
insight: 3 Opus agents (backend-architect, devops-architect, refactoring-expert) effort raised medium->high — model routing rationale (judgment tasks) must match effort level
context: Self-review flagged mismatch: Opus used for judgment but effort capped at medium. User chose option 1 (raise to high).

author: ajitta
insight: Effort/model alignment rule: Opus agents should have effort >= high. Sonnet agents should have effort <= medium. Mismatch signals configuration error
context: Derived from reviewing all 23 agents. Opus is chosen for judgment tasks that need deep reasoning — constraining effort contradicts the routing decision.

author: ajitta
insight: Leaked CC source TypeScript type shows 1-5|EffortValue union for effort field, but runtime YAML parser only accepts strings (low/medium/high/max). Type definition != runtime behavior
context: User asked to verify claim from leaked source. Web research + project history (cc-source-alignment-discovery spec SUPERSEDED note) confirm strings only at runtime.

author: ajitta
insight: Python batch script outperforms sub-agent delegation for bulk mechanical edits (52 files in seconds vs 4 failed sub-agent attempts over ~15 min)
context: fix_authoring.py handled all agent+command edits reliably. Sub-agents failed due to Edit permission issues on Windows.

author: chosh1179
insight: PostToolUse test dispatch extended by path convention (agents/commands/skills/modes .md → structure tests) over extension heuristic. Existing hook skipped .md entirely; path-based routing reuses the same hook without a second dispatcher.

author: chosh1179
insight: superclaude audit --verbose silently suppressed drift per-file details. Verbose flag wired for cross_refs/usage sections only, not drift — flag existence != verbose output coverage.

author: chosh1179
insight: Hook script sync requires both make deploy (CLI via editable) AND superclaude install --force (hook scripts). Editable install does not cover files copied to ~/.claude/superclaude/scripts/. Silent divergence source — script edits look deployed but arent active.

author: chosh1179
insight: Skipped 2b (test-driven YAML refactor loop) per [R18]. 1339/1339 structure tests already passing — no refactor backlog to iterate against. Insights report described a future workflow, not a current gap.

author: ajitta
insight: Full XML conversion of .claude/rules/*-authoring.md rejected; YAML sidecar extraction chosen.
context: 3 enum duplications + 1 red-color drift bug justify R18; 820+ lines of pedagogical prose fail necessity test.

author: ajitta
insight: Anthropic "No XML tags anywhere" ban is frontmatter-scoped only (name+description), not body-level.
context: PDF checklist wording is ambiguous tutorial guidance, not API rule. src/superclaude/** XML bodies are not violations.

author: ajitta
insight: VALID_COLORS permitted red but no agent used it and role-group table had no red entry.
context: Latent drift caught during duplication audit, not by any test. Removing red and loading colors from schemas.yaml fixed both the drift and the SSOT.

author: ajitta
insight: Sub-agent overconfident claim (Anthropic explicitly forbids) forced to NUANCED after verification challenge.
context: Always cross-verify strong external-claim findings via SendMessage + direct WebFetch before acting on sub-agent research.

author: ajitta
insight: uv run pytest fails with Failed to canonicalize script path on this Windows setup; python -m pytest works identically.
context: Use direct python when uv run misbehaves on Windows. Root cause not investigated — workaround sufficient for now.

author: ajitta
insight: --loop terminated at iteration 2. Generalized drift guard deferred (R18 YAGNI).
context: self-review flagged it as low-priority optional; current 4 guard tests cover main drift vectors with no failure evidence.

author: ajitta
insight: Rule citation precision [R06] scope-defense vs [R18] necessity-test — initial blind R18 tagging on 5 gotchas reverted to R06 in commit 10 after self-review 2nd pass.
context: content-not-service/cli-simplicity/no-frontend/rich-only/false-positive are scope-defensive (SC is not a web service/UI) not necessity-gating. R18 is for unsolicited code proposals.

author: ajitta
insight: Simulation-based audit via 5 parallel sub-agents (22 agents × 5 scenarios) reveals cross-agent inconsistencies (trigger collisions, mindset-gotcha conflicts) invisible to per-agent review.
context: 110 scenarios produced P0-P3 finding tree. Per-agent review had previously reached 100% gotcha coverage without catching cross-cutting issues.

author: ajitta
insight: install-tree-boundary near-violation — P3 Threshold Standardization originally proposed schemas.yaml extraction; user corrected mid-recommendation. .claude/rules/** is NOT shipped to ~/.claude/.
context: Runtime agents read src/superclaude/** only. Dev-time files (schemas.yaml, .claude/rules/authoring.md) are test-time enforcement only. Design pivoted to RULES.md canonical block + schemas validation test.

author: ajitta
insight: R06/R18 Uniform Citation original proposal rescoped mid-execution — strict uniformity (23/23) replaced with natural gotcha→rule mapping (20/23: R02/R06/R15/R18). 3 agents (git-workflow/insight-analyst/socratic-mentor) skipped because their gotchas dont map to framework rules.
context: Right-sized over uniform: forced tags create noise. Rescoping acknowledged in commit message, reviewer validated.

author: ajitta
insight: self-review agent is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob/Agent) — cannot run Bash tests. R15 verification must be performed by invoker; sub-agents can only do static analysis.
context: Reviewer explicitly stated Verification not possible: no Bash tool available in this review session. User ran pytest fresh and cited 831/831. Pattern: delegation ≠ verification.

author: ajitta
insight: User one-line correction (rules/schemas.yaml 이용 불가) pivoted entire P3 design. Accuracy-based user feedback is the highest-leverage loop input.
context: Initial recommendation violated install-tree-boundary gotcha (which is in .claude/rules/gotchas/authoring.md). Without user catch, P3 proposal would have produced runtime-inaccessible configuration.

author: ajitta
insight: Effort policy pivot: from model-tier alignment (Apr 12) to omit-by-default inheriting parent session. 12 agents pruned, 11 kept where domain forces deviation.
context: Parent /effort xhigh now propagates to all non-explicit agents instead of being capped by rigid medium/high frontmatter.

author: ajitta
insight: Explicit effort keep-criteria: low=mechanical (git-workflow/repo-index), high=domain-forced deep reasoning (architects/security/root-cause), max=multi-perspective synthesis. Everything else inherits.
context: medium is the default and is almost always redundant as explicit value. Codified in .claude/rules/agent-authoring.md table.

author: ajitta
insight: xhigh not adopted despite CC 2.1.111+ and Opus 4.7 support — no concrete usage evidence that existing high agents underperform.
context: Consistent with P1 A3 decision (max->xhigh downgrade also deferred without usage feedback). Add only with evidence.

author: ajitta
insight: Prior Opus>=high alignment rule (2026-04-12T02:30:04+09:00) superseded — Opus agents like refactoring-expert now omit effort entirely and rely on parent session /effort.
context: The Apr 12 rule assumed rigid per-agent effort; the new model treats parent session as SSOT for depth preference.

author: ajitta
insight: User restraint principle for frontmatter: lower if safe, remove if doubtful, add only when clearly needed. Generalizable to other optional fields.
context: User phrasing: effort를 명확하게 하양해도 성능 저하 없으면 적용, 의심스러우면 빼자, 명백히 xhigh 이상 써야 하면 넣자.

author: ajitta
insight: <mcp servers="..."> XML tag had 0 runtime effect and 0 actual usage across 23 agents — pure dead pattern.
context: agent-authoring.md template had it; audit.py parsed it; test_cross_references.py validated it. All operating on empty set. Removed from all 3 layers.

author: ajitta
insight: Rejected forcing generic <focus>/<actions> template onto 4 domain-specialized agents (deep-researcher/socratic-mentor/simplicity-guide/project-manager).
context: Domain-specific tags like <strategies>, <domains>, <hop_patterns> carry higher information density than generic template slots. Uniformity < information density when semantics differ.

author: ajitta
insight: Template-layer dead pattern removal requires 3-way cleanup: template guide + audit script + test validator.
context: Removing <mcp> from agent-authoring.md alone would leave audit.py parsing + 128 skip-tests as silent dead code. Complete removal demands all three layers coherent.

author: ajitta
insight: test_cross_references.py runtime 1.55s to 0.15s after removing 128 dead-skip MCP tests (10x speedup).
context: 128 tests always skipped (no <mcp> usage) were still instantiated by pytest fixtures. Mass-skip count is a signal of potentially removable code paths.

author: ajitta
insight: User "실측해보자" preference — treat documented claims as unverified until empirically tested.
context: Documentation said <mcp> is "Documentation-only". User demanded empirical verification, which immediately surfaced that the tag has zero usage and zero runtime effect. Proactive measurement beats post-hoc trust.
